top_articles.title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Acrobat Reader Flaw CVE-2026-34621
top_articles.summary: Adobe has released emergency updates to fix a critical security flaw in Acrobat Reader that has come under active exploitation in the wild.  The vulnerability, assigned the CVE identifier CVE-2026-346

top_articles.title: Citizen Lab: Law Enforcement Used Webloc to Track 500 Million Devices via Ad Data
top_articles.summary: Hungarian domestic intelligence, the national police in El Salvador, and several U.S. law enforcement and police departments have been attributed to the use of an advertising-based global geolocation

top_articles.title: Adobe Patches Reader Zero-Day Exploited for Months
top_articles.summary: The vulnerability is tracked as CVE-2026-34621 and Adobe has confirmed that it can be exploited for arbitrary code execution.

top_articles.title: Juniper Networks Patches Dozens of Junos OS Vulnerabilities
top_articles.summary: A critical-severity flaw could be exploited remotely, without authentication, to take over a vulnerable device.

top_articles.title: Industry Reactions to Iran Hacking ICS in Critical Infrastructure: Feedback Friday
top_articles.summary: The US government has warned that Iran-linked hackers are manipulating PLCs and SCADA systems to cause disruption.

top_articles.title: Chrome 147 Patches 60 Vulnerabilities, Including Two Critical Flaws Worth $86,000
top_articles.summary: The critical vulnerabilities affect Chrome’s WebML component and they have been reported by anonymous researchers.

top_articles.title: Marimo RCE Flaw CVE-2026-39987 Exploited Within 10 Hours of Disclosure
top_articles.summary: A critical security vulnerability in Marimo , an open-source Python notebook for data science and analysis, has been exploited within 10 hours of public disclosure, according to findings  from Sysdig.
